serStmt.serInfo: Statistics South Africa. General Household Survey 2007 [dataset]. Version 1.4. Pretoria. Statistics South Africa [producer]. 2017. Cape Town. DataFirst [distributor], 2020. DOI: https://doi.org/10.25828/0gqy-z394

verStmt.version: v1.4: Edited, anonymised dataset for public distribution
verStmt.notes:
Version 1:
Version 1 of GHS 2007 uses old weights that are no longer available for download on Statistics South Africa's website.
Version 1.2:
Version 1.2 includes new weights for comparability across GHS 2002-2008 released at the same time as GHS 2008 (in 2009). Person files were reweighted to reflect Community Survey 2007 results and update the estimates of the impact of HIV/Aids on demographic trends in South Africa. Household files were weighted independently of the person files in v1.2. The reweighting procedure was discussed in the report: "Reweighting of the GHS 2002-2008 data series".
As part of the reweighting, if individuals or household heads have missing values for age, sex or population group, these missing values were imputed. Where imputation could not be managed, these records were discarded. These demographic variables were also renamed.
v1.2 uses the new provincial boundaries for the province variable, as revised by the SA government in 2005. Mismatch in province variable between v1 and v1.2 is to be expected.
There is quality issue with the variable "uqnr" in GHS 2007 v1 files. This was fixed in v1.2.
There is quality issue with the weight variable in the person file of version 1.2. The person file that was originally given to DataFirst did not match on weight with the later revised person file of the same version.
Version 1.3:
Version 1.3 includes new weights for comparability across GHS 2002-2011 released at the same time as the GHS 2012 (22 August 2013). Reweighting was necessary in order to maintain the comparability of population estimates used in the GHS based on figures provided by the 2013 mid-year population estimation model that incorporates the demographic findings of Census 2011. Household files were weighted independently of person files.
There is quality issue with the variable "psu" in both v1 and v1.2 files for GHS 2007. This was fixed in v1.3.
Version 1.4:
Version 1.4 includes new weights for comparability across GHS 2002-2017 released at the same time as GHS 2017 (21 June 2018). It was decided to replace the 2013 series mid-year population estimation in the previous version with a the more recent 2017 series mid-year population estimation as benchmarks for weighting the GHS data files. Household files were weighted independently of person files.

stdyInfo.abstract: The GHS is an annual household survey specifically designed to measure the living circumstances of South African households. The GHS collects data on education, employment, health, housing and household access to services.

sumDscr.geogCover: The survey is representative at national level and at provincial level.
sumDscr.geogUnit: The lowest level of geographic aggregations is province.
sumDscr.universe: The survey covered all de jure household members (usual residents) of households in the nine provinces of South Africa and residents in workers' hostels. The survey does not cover collective living quarters such as students' hostels, old age homes, hospitals, prisons and military barracks.

stdyInfo.notes:
The scope of the General Household Survey includes:
Household characteristics: Dwelling type, home ownership, access to water and sanitation facilities, access to services, transport, household assets, land ownership, agricultural production
Individuals' characteristics: demographic characteristics, relationship to household head, marital status, language, education, employment, income, health, disability, access to social services, mortality.
Women's characteristics: fertility

dataColl.sampProc: The sample is multi-stage stratified using probability proportional to size principles. The first stage is stratification by province, then by type of area within each province. Primary sampling units (PSUs) are then selected proportionally within each stratum (urban or non-urban) in all provinces.
dataColl.resInstru: GHS uses questionnaires as data collection instruments

anlyInfo.dataAppr:
Earlier versions of the GHS datasets 2002 to 2007 include a District Council variable. This is no longer available in the later versions issued by Statistics SA. They caution that although the GHS 2005-2007 sample was designed to report at DC level, estimations are not reliable at this level. The 2008 - 2013 sample was designed to report at provincial and metro level. However, StatsSA did not take the absent population at metro into account when weighting the data and therefore this data is not reliable at Metro level.
The new programs that were introduced for weighting of the general household surveys from 2008 onwards, discard all records with missing values for age, sex or population group (for observations at household level, they are the values for age, sex or population group of the household head). This means that missing values of those variables were imputed. The emphasis was on obtaining reliable imputations rather than a 100% imputation rate, so some persons/households were discarded during the weighting.

useStmt.citReq: Statistics South Africa. General Household Survey 2007 [dataset]. Version 1.4. Pretoria. Statistics South Africa [producer]. 2017. Cape Town. DataFirst [distributor], 2020. DOI: https://doi.org/10.25828/0gqy-z394
useStmt.conditions: Public use files, available to all

catgry.labl: Dwelling/House or brick structure on a separate stand or yard or on farm
Traditional dwelling/Hut/Structure made of traditional materials
Flat or apartment in a block of flats
Informal dwelling/Shack not in backyard, e.g. in an informal/squatter settlement or on farm

Traditional dwelling/Hut/Structure made of traditional materials
Flat or apartment in a block of flats
Informal dwelling/Shack not in backyard, e.g. in an informal/squatter settlement or on farm

Traditional dwelling/Hut/Structure made of traditional materials
Flat or apartment in a block of flats
Informal dwelling/Shack not in backyard, e.g. in an informal/squatter settlement or on farm

Traditional dwelling/Hut/Structure made of traditional materials
Flat or apartment in a block of flats
Informal dwelling/Shack not in backyard, e.g. in an informal/squatter settlement or on farm
Owned, but not yet fully paid off (e.g. with a mortgage)
Occupied rent-free as part of employment contract of family member
Occupied rent-free not as part of employment contract of family member

var.name: Q4121Ins
var.labl: Payment on bond: Monthly instalment for the past month - Capital payment

var.name: Q415aMai
var.labl: Amount paid on: Maintenance and repair

var.name: Q415bSer
var.labl: Amount paid on: Services for maintenance and repairs

var.name: Q415dSer
var.labl: Amount paid on: services for improvements

var.name: Q415eSec
var.labl: Amount paid on: Security for improvements

catgry.labl: Piped (Tap) water on site or in yard
Piped (Tap) water on site or in yard

var.name: Q4211Saf
var.labl: Quality of drinking water: Safe to drink

var.name: Q4212Cle
var.labl: Quality of drinking water: Clear - has no colour/ free of mud

var.name: Q4213Tas
var.labl: Quality of drinking water: Good in taste

var.name: Q4214Odo
var.labl: Quality of drinking water: Free from bad smells

var.name: Q4261Met
var.labl: Why not pay for water: Irregular metering system

var.name: Q4262Nom
var.labl: Why not pay for water: No metering system in place

var.name: Q4263Bil
var.labl: Why not pay for water: Irregular billing system

var.name: Q4264Nob
var.labl: Why not pay for water: No billing system in place

var.name: Q4265Met
var.labl: Why not pay for water: Meter is broken

var.name: Q4266Cos
var.labl: Why not pay for water: Can’t afford to pay for water

var.name: Q4267unh
var.labl: Why not pay for water: Unhappy with service

var.name: Q4268Fre
var.labl: Why not pay for water: Govt must supply free water

var.name: Q4269Oth
var.labl: Why not pay for water: Others do not pay for water

var.name: Q42610FR
var.labl: Why not pay for water: Using free basic amount

var.name: Q42611OT
var.labl: Why not pay for water: Reason why not pay for water (other

var.name: Q427Inte
var.labl: Frequency of interruptions of piped water

catgry.labl: Not enough water in the system (demand too high)
Water only delivered at fixed times
Flush toilet with offsite disposal (in dwelling)
Flush toilet with offsite disposal (on site)
Flush toilet with offsite disposal (off site)
Flush toilet with on site disposal (in dwelling)
Flush toilet with on site disposal (on site)
Flush toilet with on site disposal (off site)
Pit latrine with ventilation pipe (on site)
Pit latrine without ventilation pipe (on site)
Pit latrine without ventilation pipe (off site)
Once a week or more often
Less often than once a month

var.name: Q436Elec
var.labl: Electricity cut off due to non-payment

var.name: Q438Elec
var.labl: Supply of main electricity in the past five years

catgry.labl: Removed by local authority at least once a week
Removed by local authority less often than once a week
Removed by community members at least once a week
Removed by community members less often than once a week

var.name: Q440payr
var.labl: Willing to pay for refuse service

catgry.labl: Do not know the distance to buy back centre
Do not know if there is any buy back center nearby

var.name: Q445redw
var.labl: Why household collects waster for recycling (Reduce waste)

var.name: Q445mone
var.labl: Why household collects waster for recycling (Money for recycled items)

var.name: Q445save
var.labl: Why household collects waster for recycling (To save energy/natural resources)

var.name: Q445land
var.labl: Why household collects waster for recycling (To save landfill space)

var.name: Q445redp
var.labl: Why household collects waster for recycling (To reduce litter and pollution)

var.name: Q445dkno
var.labl: Why household collects waster for recycling (Do not know)

var.name: Q447ash
var.labl: Household waste disposal (Ash, rubble and bricks)

catgry.labl: Thrown out into dustbin for refuse collection
Do not think it is important
Less than 1 large refuse removal bag
More than 3 large refuse removal bags
Less than 1 large refuse removal bag
More than 3 large refuse removal bags

var.labl: Use of telephone in the past five years

catgry.labl: Delivered to a post box/private bag

var.name: Q459Food

catgry.labl: Less than 5.000 m2 (5.000 m2 is approximately one soccer field)

var.name: Q471binh
var.labl: Been harassed/threatened by a household member

var.labl: Been sexually molested by a household member

var.name: Q471esex
var.labl: Been sexually molested by someone outside the household

var.labl: Been beaten up or hurt by a household member

var.name: Q471ghit
var.labl: Been beaten up or hurt by someone outside the household

var.name: Q471hinh
var.labl: Been murdered by a household member

var.name: Q471inot

Adult basic education and training/literacy classes

var.name: Q212NotH
var.labl: Supported by persons not in the household

var.labl: Supported by charity, church, welfare, etc.

catgry.labl: Has found a job, but is only starting at a definite date in the future
Scholar or student and prefers not to work
Housewife/homemaker and prefers not to work
Retired and prefers not to seek formal work
Illness, invalid, disabled or unable to work (handicapped)
Too young or too old to work
Seasonal worker, e.g. fruit picker, wool-shearer
Lack of skills or qualifications for available jobs
Cannot find suitable work (salary, location of work or conditions not satisfactory)
Contract worker, e.g. mine worker resting according to contract
Later than four weeks from now
Financial intermediation, insurance, real estate and business services
Service workers and shop and market sales workers
Plant and machine operators and assemblers
